introSection: Unkonwn is a steel inverted roller coaster currently under construction at Wonderla Amusement Park Bangalore in Bangalore, Karnataka, India. The ride was previously known as T3 (pronounced "T-three" or "Terror to the Third Power") and was located at Kentucky Kingdom in Louisville, Kentucky, USA. Opened in 1995, T3 was the second of two prototype Suspended Looping Coasters built by Vekoma. It only has seven cars per train, as opposed to the usual ten found on other SLCs.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride was originally announced as T2 on December 1, 1994. It opened on April 8, 1995.

In 1999, the ride was announced to be rethemed to Batman, with T2 being renamed to "Batman: The Ride" and Chang to "Riddler's Revenge." These plans were later cancelled.

T2 closed with the park in 2009 due to Six Flags' bankruptcy. Kentucky Kingdom reopened on May 24, 2014 under new ownership, but T2 remained closed. It was later announced that T2 would be overhauled and renamed T3 for the 2015 season.

T3 reopened on July 3, 2015, after receiving new modern-style vest restraints with soft straps and a bar that sits on riders' legs after being renovated at a cost of $2 million. The ride was also repainted entirely red.

T3 last operated on October 30, 2022. On April 27, 2023, Kentucky Kingdom announced that T3 would not reopen. The ride was later listed for sale on Amusement-Rides.com. The ride was removed from the park in spring 2024. The ride was then sold to Wonderla Holidays Limited and was shipped off to Wonderla Amusement Park Bangalore in India. As of December 2025, the ride is being rebuilt at its new location.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Red track and supports. Opened with red track and supports, later changed to feature black track and supports. For its reopening in 2015, the color scheme was reverted back to the original all-red scheme.

fullText:
Unkonwn is a steel inverted roller coaster currently under construction at Wonderla Amusement Park Bangalore in Bangalore, Karnataka, India. The ride was previously known as T3 (pronounced "T-three" or "Terror to the Third Power") and was located at Kentucky Kingdom in Louisville, Kentucky, USA. Opened in 1995, T3 was the second of two prototype Suspended Looping Coasters built by Vekoma. It only has seven cars per train, as opposed to the usual ten found on other SLCs.

The ride was originally announced as T2 on December 1, 1994. It opened on April 8, 1995.

In 1999, the ride was announced to be rethemed to Batman, with T2 being renamed to "Batman: The Ride" and Chang to "Riddler's Revenge." These plans were later cancelled.

T2 closed with the park in 2009 due to Six Flags' bankruptcy. Kentucky Kingdom reopened on May 24, 2014 under new ownership, but T2 remained closed. It was later announced that T2 would be overhauled and renamed T3 for the 2015 season.

T3 reopened on July 3, 2015, after receiving new modern-style vest restraints with soft straps and a bar that sits on riders' legs after being renovated at a cost of $2 million. The ride was also repainted entirely red.

T3 last operated on October 30, 2022. On April 27, 2023, Kentucky Kingdom announced that T3 would not reopen. The ride was later listed for sale on Amusement-Rides.com. The ride was removed from the park in spring 2024. The ride was then sold to Wonderla Holidays Limited and was shipped off to Wonderla Amusement Park Bangalore in India. As of December 2025, the ride is being rebuilt at its new location.

See Suspended Looping Coaster.

Red track and supports. Opened with red track and supports, later changed to feature black track and supports. For its reopening in 2015, the color scheme was reverted back to the original all-red scheme.






 * T3 on the parks website
